text: Delivered (commit b35d7fd; before/after in benchmarks/results/2026-07-10-breeze-tuned.md). Kernel changes: 4-lane ddot + 4x daxpy contiguous fast paths, unrolled row-major gemv, 4x4 register-tiled gemm micro-kernel (gemmPanel, C in 16 accumulators across k) in direct+blocked paths, GemmBlock retuned 64->128 (measured: n=256 gemm 267->356 ops/s), assign-only dsyrkRowMajor for a.t*a via structural isTransposeOf gate (reviewed: false positives algebraically impossible). Work split: implementer agent did L1/L2/syrk/micro-kernel; lead did block retune, review nits (all-tail gemm test, syrk assign-only doc), verification. RESULTS vs pure-Java Breeze: dot 0.29-0.38 -> 0.77-0.98 (parity band), gemv 0.29-0.50 -> 0.95-0.98, AtA 0.12-0.26 -> 0.36-0.77, gemm 0.39-0.51 -> 0.70-0.77, axpy 0.38 -> 0.63-0.68; norm (3-5x) and small-n factorization/eigen wins preserved; eigSym-128 0.68 -> 0.81. ACCEPTANCE BAR (majority >= breeze at largest size) NOT met — honest status: parity band on the compute kernels, but large-n QR (0.23x) and least-squares (0.27x) are an ALGORITHM gap (unblocked Householder vs LAPACK blocked/WY), not a kernel tune; axpy is store-bound; Solve/Chol-256 numbers ran under background load (re-measure before acting). Follow-up bead created: gale-qr-blocked-householder.
